topic: Correlate suspicious process execution with immediate C2 network connection

content.question: Write a KQL query against Microsoft Defender XDR tables to identify devices where a suspicious process (powershell.exe, cmd.exe, mshta.exe, wscript.exe, or cscript.exe spawned by an Office application parent) made an outbound public-IP network connection within 120 seconds of its creation. Return the device name, initiating process, child process command line, remote IP, remote port, and the seconds elapsed between process creation and first network connection. Sort by elapsed time ascending (fastest C2 check-in first).
content.sample_solution:
let officeApps = dynamic(["WINWORD.EXE", "EXCEL.EXE", "POWERPNT.EXE", "OUTLOOK.EXE"]);
let suspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe", "cmd.exe", "mshta.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe"]);
let suspiciousProcs = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (officeApps)
| where FileName has_any (suspiciousChildren)
| project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ChildProcess = FileName,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ProcessId;
let netEvents = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort,
          InitiatingProcessId;
suspiciousProcs
| join kind=inner netEvents
    on DeviceName, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where NetTime >= ProcTime
| extend ElapsedSeconds = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, ProcTime)
| where ElapsedSeconds <= 120
| project ProcTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentProcess,
          ChildProcess, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ElapsedSeconds
| sort by ElapsedSeconds asc

topic: Join IdentityLogonEvents with DeviceProcessEvents to detect logon-to-command-execution chains

content.question: Write a KQL query that joins IdentityLogonEvents (Defender Identity) with DeviceProcessEvents to find cases where an interactive or remote-interactive NTLM logon is followed within 5 minutes by execution of a shell process (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or net.exe) on the same device, under the same account. This pattern indicates post-exploitation command execution immediately after credential-based lateral movement. Return account, device, logon time, first command executed, and minutes elapsed.
content.sample_solution:
let lateralLogons = IdentityLogonEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where LogonType in ("Interactive", "RemoteInteractive")
| where Protocol == "Ntlm"
| where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
| project LogonTime = Timestamp,
          AccountUpn,
          TargetDevice = DestinationDeviceName;
lateralLogons
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(24h)
    | where FileName has_any ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "net.exe", "whoami.exe", "ipconfig.exe")
    | project ExecTime = Timestamp,
              DeviceName,
              AccountName,
              FileName,
              ProcessCommandLine
) on $left.TargetDevice == $right.DeviceName
| where ExecTime > LogonTime
| extend MinutesElapsed = datetime_diff('minute', ExecTime, LogonTime)
| where MinutesElapsed <= 5
| summarize
    FirstCommand = arg_min(ExecTime, ProcessCommandLine),
    CommandCount = count()
    by AccountUpn, TargetDevice, LogonTime, MinutesElapsed
| project LogonTime, AccountUpn, TargetDevice, MinutesElapsed,
          FirstCommand, CommandCount
| sort by LogonTime desc

topic: Detect C2 beacon jitter using bin_at and coefficient-of-variation analysis

content.question: Write a KQL query against DeviceNetworkEvents to detect C2 beaconing using statistical regularity of connection intervals. For each (DeviceName, RemoteIP) pair connecting to public IPs, compute the connection interval series, then calculate the average interval and standard deviation. Flag pairs with 6 or more connections, average interval between 60 and 3600 seconds, and a coefficient of variation (StdDev / Mean) below 0.25 — indicating highly regular timing consistent with beacon jitter. Use bin_at to align time bins to a fixed reference point. Return device, remote IP, connection count, average interval, and jitter percentage.
content.sample_solution:
let fixedRef = datetime(2026-01-01T00:00:00Z);
let binSize = 1m;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(48h)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| summarize ConnectionCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            BinCount = dcount(bin_at(Timestamp, binSize, fixedRef))
    by DeviceName, RemoteIP
| where ConnectionCount >= 6
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(48h)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
    | summarize ConnectionTimes = make_list(Timestamp, 500)
        by DeviceName, RemoteIP
    | mv-expand ConnectionTimes to typeof(datetime)
    | sort by DeviceName asc, RemoteIP asc, ConnectionTimes asc
    | serialize
    | extend PrevTime = prev(ConnectionTimes, 1)
    | extend PrevDevice = prev(DeviceName, 1)
    | extend PrevIP = prev(RemoteIP, 1)
    | where DeviceName == PrevDevice and RemoteIP == PrevIP
    | extend IntervalSec = datetime_diff('second', ConnectionTimes, PrevTime)
    | where IntervalSec > 0
    | summarize AvgInterval = avg(IntervalSec), StdDevInterval = stdev(IntervalSec)
        by DeviceName, RemoteIP
) on DeviceName, RemoteIP
| where AvgInterval between (60 .. 3600)
| extend CoeffVariation = StdDevInterval / AvgInterval
| where CoeffVariation < 0.25
| project DeviceName, RemoteIP, ConnectionCount,
          AvgIntervalSec = round(AvgInterval, 0),
          JitterPct = round(CoeffVariation * 100, 1)
| sort by JitterPct asc

topic: Extract and decode Base64 encoded PowerShell payloads using parse and mv-expand

content.question: Write a KQL query against DeviceProcessEvents to find PowerShell processes launched with -EncodedCommand, extract the Base64 payload using extract(), decode it using base64_decode_tostring(), parse out any embedded URLs using mv-expand on a split of the decoded string, and return the device name, account, encoded payload, decoded script, and any URL-like tokens found in the decoded content. Filter to the past 24 hours.
content.sample_solution:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(24h)
| where FileName =~ "powershell.exe" or FileName =~ "pwsh.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand", "-enc ", "-ec ")
| extend EncodedBlob = extract(@"-(?:EncodedCommand|enc|ec)\s+([A-Za-z0-9+/=]+)",
    1, ProcessCommandLine)
| where isnotempty(EncodedBlob)
| extend DecodedScript = base64_decode_tostring(EncodedBlob)
| where isnotempty(DecodedScript)
| parse DecodedScript with * "://" UrlSuffix
| mv-expand Tokens = split(DecodedScript, ' ') to typeof(string)
| where Tokens startswith "http://" or Tokens startswith "https://"
    or Tokens startswith "ftp://"
| summarize
    C2Candidates = make_set(Tokens, 20),
    SampleDecoded = any(DecodedScript),
    EncodedPayload = any(EncodedBlob),
    CmdLine = any(ProcessCommandLine)
    by Timestamp = bin(Timestamp, 1h), DeviceName, AccountName
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          CmdLine, EncodedPayload, SampleDecoded, C2Candidates
| sort by Timestamp desc

topic: Materialize suspicious dropped files and hunt cross-table for execution and C2

content.question: Write a KQL query using let + materialize() to first build a set of suspicious file hashes (executables dropped to Temp or AppData directories in the past 7 days from DeviceFileEvents), then join that materialized set against DeviceProcessEvents to find confirmed executions of those suspicious files, and finally join against DeviceNetworkEvents to find any C2 network connections made by those processes within 10 minutes of execution. Return SHA256, file name, affected device count, execution count, and unique C2 IPs contacted.
content.sample_solution:
let lookback = 7d;
let suspiciousDropped = materialize(
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\Downloads\")
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll"
        or FileName endswith ".ps1"
    | where SHA256 != ""
    | distinct SHA256, FileName
);
let executions = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where SHA256 in ((suspiciousDropped | project SHA256))
| project ExecTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          SHA256, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId;
let networkHits = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessId,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort;
executions
| join kind=leftouter networkHits
    on DeviceName, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where isnull(NetTime) or datetime_diff('minute', NetTime, ExecTime) <= 10
| summarize
    AffectedDevices = dcount(DeviceName),
    ExecutionCount = count(),
    C2IPs = make_set(RemoteIP, 50)
    by SHA256, FileName
| where ExecutionCount >= 1
| extend HasC2 = array_length(C2IPs) > 0
| sort by AffectedDevices desc, ExecutionCount desc
